title: Contains Duplicate
performanceTarget: top 1% execution time and overall performance
note: C proposal for this challenge: the minimum-memory champion. Recommended and speed proposals are in C++ (see crossReferences).

variants.languageReason: Smallest runtime/stdlib baseline among the allowed languages -> minimum reported memory; manual control enables an in-place, allocation-free sort.
variants.approachJustification: In-place qsort + adjacent-equality scan, overflow-safe comparator.

reasoningSummary: In-place sort brings equal values adjacent with zero auxiliary memory - the minimum-memory way to detect duplicates given the wide value range.
